title: Gentlea micranthera
intro: Gentlea micranthera, also known as Indian gentian, is a flowering plant native to the Himalayas and other mountainous regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and fever, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gentlea micranthera include gentiopicrin, gentianine, and amarogentin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Gentlea micranthera is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in the production of traditional Indian medicines such as Ayurvedic and Unani preparations.
